title: Ukraine Strikes Tuapse Refinery for Third Time — Evacuations Ordered in Krasnodar
detail: Ukrainian drones struck the Tuapse oil refinery in Russia's Krasnodar region for the third time in two weeks on April 28, triggering evacuations of residents near the Rosneft facility. Black smoke from the blaze was visible from Sochi, 80 km to the south. Emergency authorities deployed over 300 firefighting personnel. The refinery processes approximately 12 million metric tons of crude oil annually and is a key export hub for naphtha, fuel oil, and diesel — making it a high-value target for Ukraine's long-range drone campaign against Russian energy infrastructure. Ukrainian drones also struck oil facilities in Perm and Orsk as part of the same escalating campaign against Russia's petroleum sector, which directly funds its war machine.

media.caption: Ukraine's third strike on Tuapse refinery forces evacuations; black smoke visible from Sochi

title: Ukrainian SOF Drones Destroy Iskander Missile Storage Base in Occupied Crimea
detail: Ukraine's Special Operations Forces (SOF) launched drone strikes overnight on April 28, hitting a storage base for Russian Iskander operational-tactical missile systems in occupied Crimea. The facility, located near the village of Ovrazhki approximately 40 km from Simferopol on the grounds of a former Soviet missile base, housed Iskander missiles capable of striking Ukrainian frontlines or rear cities within minutes. The strike set off fires and secondary explosions at the storage depot, significantly disrupting Russia's Iskander missile supply chain for the southern theater. Ukraine's sustained campaign against Russian missile and ammunition storage in Crimea aims to reduce Russia's capacity to conduct large-scale strikes against Ukrainian cities.

media.caption: Ukrainian SOF drones hit Iskander missile storage base near Simferopol in occupied Crimea
